title: mentha × villosa var. nemorosa
intro: Mentha × villosa var. nemorosa, also known as American wild bergamot, is a variety of mint native to North America. It is a perennial plant known for its fragrant, pink or purple flowers and its essential oil, which contains compounds like linalool and geraniol. The medicinal properties of American wild bergamot include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory infections. The health benefits of this plant include reducing stress, improving digestion, and alleviating symptoms of anxiety and depression, and it can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils.
